title: cardamine resedifolia subsp. hamulosa
intro: Cardamine resedifolia subsp. hamulosa, also known as alpine bittercress or hamulosa cardamine, is a perennial plant species in the family Brassicaceae. It is characterized by its small white flowers and heart-shaped leaves, and is often found in mountainous regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in treating wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Cardamine resedifolia subsp. hamulosa include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to soothe and protect the skin, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
